title: Claude API Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using the Claude API for AI chatbot development.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative writing or brainstorming ideas.

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in the AI's response.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to save on API costs.

commands.description: Selects the Claude-2 model for processing requests.
commands.scenario: When you require the latest model for improved accuracy and performance.

commands.description: Enables streaming of responses for real-time applications.
commands.scenario: When building a chatbot that needs to respond quickly to user input.

commands.description: Simplifies complex explanations for better understanding.
commands.scenario: When the AI needs to explain technical concepts to non-experts.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages logical reasoning and verification of results.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require detailed analysis.

commands.description: Sets the size of the context window for the AI's memory.
commands.scenario: When handling large conversations or documents that need more context.

commands.description: Formats the response in bullet points for better readability.
commands.scenario: When the output needs to be easily digestible and organized.

commands.description: Provides detailed logs of the API's processing steps.
commands.scenario: When debugging or optimizing the API's performance.

commands.description: Generates a summary of the provided text input.
commands.scenario: When you need a quick overview of a long document.

commands.description: Translates the input text to French.
commands.scenario: When developing a multilingual chatbot that supports French.

commands.description: Generates a list of advantages and disadvantages.
commands.scenario: When evaluating different options or making decisions.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authenticating requests.
commands.scenario: When initializing the API client to start making requests.

commands.description: Offers a counterargument to the given statement.
commands.scenario: When exploring different perspectives in a discussion.

commands.description: Creates a Python code snippet based on the given description.
commands.scenario: When building a coding assistant that provides code examples.

commands.command: system_prompt="You are a senior DevOps engineer..."
commands.description: Use the system parameter to define persona, constraints, and output format independently of the user message for better instruction adherence.
Adjust nucleus sampling to control the diversity of the output by limiting the cumulative probability of the next token selection.
Wrap large datasets or instructions in XML tags to help Claude distinguish between instructions and data, reducing prompt injection and confusion.
Define specific strings that signal the model to cease generation, preventing 'hallucinated' dialogue or trailing text.
Provide 2-3 completed examples of the desired task within the prompt to drastically improve pattern recognition and formatting accuracy.
